definition: Regulation (EU) 2024/1781, establishing a framework for setting ecodesign requirements for products to improve their environmental sustainability and circularity.
An electronic record containing product-specific information related to sustainability, circularity, and other environmental aspects, mandated by Article 9 of the ESPR Regulation.
Legal acts adopted by the European Commission to supplement or amend non-essential parts of legislative acts, used in ESPR to define product-specific requirements.
A physical or digital medium (e.g., QR code, RFID tag) used to provide access to the Digital Product Passport information.
South Africa's sovereign Digital Product Passport infrastructure (digitalproductpassports.co.za), designed to help South African exporters comply with ESPR Article 11 registry requirements.
An EU policy designed to prevent carbon leakage by imposing a carbon price on imports of certain carbon-intensive goods.
Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 concerning the Registration, Evaluation, Authorisation and Restriction of Chemicals, aiming to protect human health and the environment.
The predecessor to the ESPR, primarily focused on improving the energy efficiency of energy-related products.
